message:
feat(installer-core): introduce acfs_*_resolve_{current_user,getent_passwd_entry,passwd_home_from_entry} helpers

Reshape how the top-of-install bootstrap layer resolves the acting
Unix user and the associated $HOME when the ambient environment is
hostile (root in an empty env, a running Dockerfile with no TTY,
Ubuntu 26.04 paths where /usr/bin/getent has moved, a broken $PATH
that masks id/whoami with a shell builtin, etc.).

Surface shape, consistent across all three bootstrap entry points in
this commit:

- `acfs_early_system_binary_path <name>` — walk the canonical sbin/bin
  absolute paths in order and echo the first executable match. Skips
  anything that fails a conservative `[A-Za-z0-9._+-]*` name-filter
  regex so a malicious $1 can't drive an arbitrary-exec.
- `acfs_early_resolve_current_user` — prefers `id -un`, falls back to
  `whoami`, never invokes the shell builtin of either.
- `acfs_early_getent_passwd_entry [user]` — prefers `getent passwd`,
  but now also degrades cleanly to a pure-bash `/etc/passwd` scan when
  `getent` is missing (certain minimal container images) or when it
  returns a rc!=0 that isn't a user-not-found. With no argument, dumps
  the full passwd table using the same precedence.
- `acfs_early_passwd_home_from_entry <passwd_entry>` — splits on `:`
  using bash's own IFS expansion (no external `cut`), filters out root
  (`/`) and non-absolute values, and strips a trailing slash before
  echoing the home column.

This is the same surface that lives in `scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh`
for interactive shells and in per-script prefixed variants
(`onboard_*`, `state_*`, `cheatsheet_*`) for standalone entry points.
The three copies can't trivially be dedup'd: install.sh runs before
anything can be sourced, and the generated installers run before
`scripts/lib/*` is guaranteed to be on disk.

Changes in this commit:

- `install.sh`: add `acfs_early_*` helpers at the top of the bootstrap
  (right after the `$HOME/.local/bin` export), and have every
  bootstrap-phase target-user / target-home resolution step call those
  helpers instead of raw `getent passwd "$USER" | cut -d: -f6`.
- `packages/manifest/src/generate.ts`: emit an `acfs_generated_*`
  copy of the same helper set into every generated installer prelude
  so the generated scripts have their own drop-in resolvers without
  having to source install_helpers.sh.
- `packages/manifest/src/generate.test.ts`: cover the new generated
  prelude shape.
- `acfs.manifest.yaml`: replace inline `getent passwd ... | cut -d: -f6`
  payloads in affected modules (target-home probe, interactive shell
  switcher, etc.) with `acfs_generated_getent_passwd_entry` +
  `acfs_generated_passwd_home_from_entry` + `acfs_generated_resolve_current_user`
  calls. Also add an explicit post-resolution guard that refuses to
  accept a resolved `target_home` that is empty, `/`, or non-absolute,
  so a malformed /etc/passwd row can't get the installer to chmod or
  chown the filesystem root.
- `scripts/generated/*`: regenerated via `cd packages/manifest &&
  bun run generate`, so every generated installer (`install_all.sh`,
  `install_acfs.sh`, `install_agents.sh`, `install_base.sh`,
  `install_cli.sh`, `install_cloud.sh`, `install_db.sh`,
  `install_filesystem.sh`, `install_lang.sh`, `install_network.sh`,
  `install_shell.sh`, `install_stack.sh`, `install_tools.sh`,
  `install_users.sh`, `doctor_checks.sh`, `manifest_index.sh`,
  `internal_checksums.sh`) picks up the new `acfs_generated_*`
  prelude and the hardened target-home guard from the manifest.
- `scripts/generated/internal_checksums.sh`: updated checksums for
  `scripts/lib/{export-config,state,doctor,install_helpers,agents,os_detect,update,user}.sh`
  plus `scripts/{acfs-global,acfs-update}` to reflect the library-side
  migration landing alongside this change (next commit). Resolves the
  auto-generated drift fix that landed on origin/main while this
  branch was dirty (commit 6487e423).

No behavior change on systems where `id`, `whoami`, and `getent` are
all present in their canonical absolute paths; the new resolvers are
a strict superset of the old behavior with absolute-path hardening,
$PATH-independence, and graceful degradation when any single tool is
absent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 # This is critical because the Claude native installer puts the binary at ~/.local/bin/claude
 export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
 
+acfs_early_system_binary_path() {
+    local name="${1:-}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    [[ -n "$name" ]] || return 1
+    case "$name" in
+        *[!A-Za-z0-9._+-]*)
+            return 1
+            ;;
+    esac
+
+    for candidate in \
+        "/usr/local/bin/$name" \
+        "/usr/bin/$name" \
+        "/bin/$name" \
+        "/usr/sbin/$name" \
+        "/sbin/$name"; do
+        [[ -x "$candidate" ]] || continue
+        echo "$candidate"
+        return 0
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+acfs_early_resolve_current_user() {
+    local current_user=""
+    local id_bin=""
+    local whoami_bin=""
+
+    id_bin="$(acfs_early_system_binary_path id 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$id_bin" ]]; then
+        current_user="$("$id_bin" -un 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$current_user" ]]; then
+        whoami_bin="$(acfs_early_system_binary_path whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$whoami_bin" ]]; then
+            current_user="$("$whoami_bin" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    [[ -n "$current_user" ]] || return 1
+    echo "$current_user"
+}
+
+acfs_early_getent_passwd_entry() {
+    local user="${1:-}"
+    local getent_bin=""
+    local passwd_line=""
+    local passwd_user=""
+
+    getent_bin="$(acfs_early_system_binary_path getent 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$getent_bin" ]]; then
+        if [[ -n "$user" ]]; then
+            "$getent_bin" passwd "$user" 2>/dev/null
+        else
+            "$getent_bin" passwd 2>/dev/null
+        fi
+        return $?
+    fi
+
+    [[ -r /etc/passwd ]] || return 1
+
+    if [[ -n "$user" ]]; then
+        while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+            IFS=: read -r passwd_user _ <<< "$passwd_line"
+            if [[ "$passwd_user" == "$user" ]]; then
+                echo "$passwd_line"
+                return 0
+            fi
+        done < /etc/passwd
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+        echo "$passwd_line"
+    done < /etc/passwd
+}
 # Default options
 YES_MODE=false
 DRY_RUN=false

@@ -4023,16 +4124,25 @@ EOF

 fi
 
+acfs_generated_system_binary_path() {
+    local name="\${1:-}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    [[ -n "\$name" ]] || return 1
+
+    for candidate in \
+        "/usr/local/bin/\$name" \
+        "/usr/bin/\$name" \
+        "/bin/\$name" \
+        "/usr/sbin/\$name" \
+        "/sbin/\$name"
+    do
+        [[ -x "\$candidate" ]] || continue
+        printf '%s\n' "\$candidate"
+        return 0
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+acfs_generated_resolve_current_user() {
+    local current_user=""
+    local id_bin=""
+    local whoami_bin=""
+
+    id_bin="\$(acfs_generated_system_binary_path id 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "\$id_bin" ]]; then
+        current_user="\$("\$id_bin" -un 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "\$current_user" ]]; then
+        whoami_bin="\$(acfs_generated_system_binary_path whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "\$whoami_bin" ]]; then
+            current_user="\$("\$whoami_bin" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    [[ -n "\$current_user" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "\$current_user"
+}
+
+acfs_generated_getent_passwd_entry() {
+    local user="\${1-}"
+    local getent_bin=""
+    local passwd_entry=""
+    local passwd_line=""
+    local printed_any=false
+
+    getent_bin="\$(acfs_generated_system_binary_path getent 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -z "\$user" ]]; then
+        if [[ -n "\$getent_bin" ]]; then
+            while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+                printf '%s\n' "\$passwd_line"
+                printed_any=true
+            done < <("\$getent_bin" passwd 2>/dev/null || true)
+            if [[ "\$printed_any" == true ]]; then
+                return 0
+            fi
+        fi
+
+        [[ -r /etc/passwd ]] || return 1
+        while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+            printf '%s\n' "\$passwd_line"
+        done < /etc/passwd
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "\$getent_bin" ]]; then
+        passwd_entry="\$("\$getent_bin" passwd "\$user" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "\$passwd_entry" ]] && [[ -r /etc/passwd ]]; then
+        while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+            [[ "\${passwd_line%%:*}" == "\$user" ]] || continue
+            passwd_entry="\$passwd_line"
+            break
+        done < /etc/passwd
+    fi
+
+    [[ -n "\$passwd_entry" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "\$passwd_entry"
+}
+
+acfs_generated_passwd_home_from_entry() {
+    local passwd_entry="\${1:-}"
+    local passwd_home=""
+
+    [[ -n "\$passwd_entry" ]] || return 1
+    IFS=: read -r _ _ _ _ _ passwd_home _ <<< "\$passwd_entry"
+    if [[ -n "\$passwd_home" ]] && [[ "\$passwd_home" == /* ]] && [[ "\$passwd_home" != "/" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "\${passwd_home%/}"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
 # When running a generated installer directly (not sourced by install.sh),
 # set sane defaults and derive ACFS paths from the script location so
 # contract validation passes and local assets are discoverable.
@@ -80,7 +178,14 @@ if [[ "\${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" = "\${0}" ]]; then
         if [[ \$EUID -eq 0 ]] && [[ -z "\${SUDO_USER:-}" ]]; then
             _ACFS_DETECTED_USER="ubuntu"
         else
-            _ACFS_DETECTED_USER="\${SUDO_USER:-\$(whoami)}"
+            _ACFS_DETECTED_USER="\${SUDO_USER:-}"
+            if [[ -z "\$_ACFS_DETECTED_USER" ]]; then
+                _ACFS_DETECTED_USER="\$(acfs_generated_resolve_current_user 2>/dev/null || true)"
+            fi
+            if [[ -z "\$_ACFS_DETECTED_USER" ]]; then
+                log_error "Unable to resolve the current user for TARGET_USER"
+                exit 1
